serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake as served at Starbucks Singapore weighs approximately 120 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, chocolate, commercially prepared with chocolate frosting (SR Legacy 18101) adapted for pistachio-strawberry layer cake; cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods pistachio (FDC ID 170184) and strawberry (FDC ID 167762) components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake. Values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA SR Legacy commercially prepared layer cake (18101), pistachio nuts (170184), and strawberry (167762), adjusted for typical cream-filled layer cake proportions. Energy and macros are consistent with comparable commercial layer cakes in USDA Branded Foods category. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Cake, sponge (GI ~46) and layer cake with icing (GI ~38–67 range); analogous estimate for pistachio-strawberry cream layer cake set at GI 62 consistent with commercially prepared cream-filled layer cakes.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for commercially prepared layer cakes and cream-filled sponge cakes (GI range 38–67). Pistachio and strawberry components have low individual GI values (pistachio GI ~15, strawberry GI ~40) but the dominant refined flour and sugar matrix drives the overall GI into the medium range. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (62 × 47.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 33.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg/100 g) and high sugar load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a standard slice, as the combined sodium (approximately 252 mg per 120 g serving) and high refined sugar content may contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content (47 g carbohydrate, 30 g sugar per 100 g) with medium GI of 62
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cake as an occasional indulgence only; a standard 120 g slice delivers approximately 56 g of carbohydrate and 36 g of sugar, producing a glycemic load of ~33, which requires careful insulin or medication adjustment and should not be consumed without accounting for total daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (7.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume this cake sparingly; the saturated fat content (approximately 9.4 g per 120 g serving) approaches or exceeds the recommended daily limit for saturated fat in cardiac diets, and the trans fat and high sugar content further elevate atherogenic risk with regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake is a premium Western-style layer cake sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets, including the One Fullerton location. The cake features moist pistachio-flavoured sponge layers that derive their characteristic nutty richness from ground pistachio nuts incorporated into the batter. The layers are filled and frosted with cream, and the cake is finished with praline — caramelised pistachio or mixed-nut brittle — which adds a crunchy, toffee-like textural contrast. Fresh or preserved strawberries provide a natural tartness that balances the sweetness of the praline and the richness of the cream, resulting in a refreshing flavour profile. The cake is baked, then assembled and chilled before service. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense dessert at approximately 370 kcal per 100 g, with a significant sugar content of 30 g and fat content of 18.5 g per 100 g. The medium glycemic index of approximately 62 means it produces a moderate-to-high glycemic response, making it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. Pistachio contributes small amounts of plant protein, healthy monounsaturated fats, vitamin E, and magnesium. Strawberry contributes vitamin C and antioxidants. The cake is vegetarian and assumed halal based on Starbucks Singapore's standard product certification, but is not vegan, gluten-free, or dairy-free. It is best enjoyed as an occasional treat and pairs well with coffee or tea.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Starbucks Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake?
Is the Starbucks pistachio cake halal?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Strawberry Pistachio Praline Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA SR Legacy commercially prepared layer cake, pistachio, and strawberry entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for comparable cream-filled layer cakes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the estimation methodology is systematic and cross-referenced against multiple approved USDA sources.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1.0 because it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy layer cake reference entry and is not expected to be a significant nutrient in this product. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are estimated from USDA component data and should be treated as indicative rather than analytically confirmed. The price of SGD 9.30 is preserved from the field survey data source record. Starbucks Singapore halal certification status should be verified against the current Muis (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore) certified list before clinical or dietary use. The cuisine tag 'Sandwich' in the original input record appears to be a field survey platform categorisation artefact and has not been carried forward as a cuisine descriptor for this cake product.
